siteSettings.title: SS-10 ScaleStick™ Maintenance SOP
siteSettings.description: Interactive training site for the SS-10 ScaleStick cartridge replacement standard operating procedure. Covers safety protocols, step-by-step procedure simulation, compliance assessment, and field verification logging.

content.executiveSummary: The SS-10 ScaleStick™ is a commercial-grade scale inhibition system designed for water treatment applications in food service, hospitality, and industrial environments. Proper maintenance of the filtration cartridge is critical to system performance, equipment longevity, and regulatory compliance. This training module provides the complete standard operating procedure for cartridge replacement, including system isolation, cartridge extraction, O-ring verification, and system recommissioning.
safetyHazards: High-pressure water system (up to 125 PSI) — risk of forceful water discharge
Chemical exposure from scale inhibitor media and residual water treatment compounds
Slipping hazard from water discharge during housing removal
Pinch points during housing reassembly if cross-threaded
requiredTools: SS-10 replacement filter cartridge (verify model compatibility)
Filter housing wrench (spanner type, included with unit)
Replacement O-ring gasket (3.5" ID x 4.0" OD)
Clean lint-free cloths or shop towels
Adjustable wrench (for inlet/outlet fittings if needed)

phase1.title: System Isolation & Depressurization
steps: Close the inlet valve — rotate upstream shutoff clockwise until fully closed
Relieve system pressure — open the pressure relief button/valve
Open the nearest downstream fixture to bleed remaining pressure
Verify depressurization — gauge must read 0 PSI
Position drainage pan beneath the filter housing

phase2.title: Cartridge Extraction
steps: Unscrew the filter housing using the spanner wrench (counter-clockwise)
Remove the housing — pull straight down carefully
Extract the old cartridge — lift straight up, note flow direction
Inspect the housing interior for cracks or deposits
Inspect the O-ring seat for debris or scoring

phase3.title: New Cartridge Installation & O-Ring Verification
steps: Inspect the new cartridge — verify model number and condition
Remove and inspect the existing O-ring
Lubricate the O-ring with food-grade silicone
Seat the O-ring correctly — flat, no twists or pinches
Insert the new cartridge with flow arrow pointing UP
Hand-tighten the housing — 1/4 turn past finger-tight, NO wrench

phase4.title: System Recommissioning
steps: Close the relief valve
SLOWLY open the inlet valve — no more than 1/4 turn initially
Purge trapped air — wait until steady flow with no sputtering
Fully open the inlet valve once air is purged
Check for leaks — inspect all connections for 5 minutes
Verify system pressure — should return to normal range (40-80 PSI)
Record the service in the Field Verification Log

quizQuestions.question: What is the primary operational consequence of mineral scale build-up inside commercial water lines?
options: Decreases water pressure to 0 PSI immediately
Acts as a thermal insulator, increasing energy consumption and causing potential equipment failure
Changes the water taste to highly acidic
Increases filter cartridge lifespan by trapping sediment
quizQuestions.explanation: Scale acts as a thermal insulator which reduces heating efficiency and blocks water lines, causing failure.

quizQuestions.question: Before removing the ScaleStick housing, which step must be completed FIRST?
options: Apply O-ring lubricant to the housing threads
Open the downstream fixture to full flow
Verify the system is fully depressurized (0 PSI on gauge)
Position the replacement cartridge within reach
quizQuestions.explanation: System depressurization is the critical safety step that must be confirmed before any disassembly.

quizQuestions.question: When inserting the new filter cartridge, which direction should the flow arrow point?
options: Downward (toward the sump housing)
Upward (toward the cap/head unit)
The arrow direction does not matter
Sideways (toward the inlet port)
quizQuestions.explanation: The flow arrow must point UP toward the cap to ensure proper water flow through the filtration media.

quizQuestions.question: How should the housing be tightened during reassembly?
options: Use the spanner wrench to tighten as firmly as possible
Hand-tighten only — approximately 1/4 turn past finger-tight
Use an adjustable wrench for maximum torque
Tighten until you hear the O-ring compress
quizQuestions.explanation: Over-tightening with tools risks cross-threading and O-ring damage. Hand-tightening provides adequate seal.

quizQuestions.question: During recommissioning, why is the inlet valve opened slowly?
options: To prevent the cartridge from shifting out of position
To allow gradual pressurization and controlled air purge without water hammer
To give the O-ring time to expand
To test if the downstream fixture is working
quizQuestions.explanation: Gradual opening prevents water hammer damage and allows trapped air to purge safely.
